skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem:
4x+5y=11

The given equation is one equation in a system of two linear equations. If the system of equations has at least one solution, which of the following equations could be the other equation in the system?
rationale: Choice C is correct. For a linear system to have at least one solution, the second equation must either be equivalent to the first (infinitely many solutions) or have a different slope (one solution). Choice C, 8x + 10y = 22, is exactly twice the given equation (4x + 5y = 11), so it represents the same line — infinitely many solutions. Choices A, B, and D all have proportional coefficients to the given equation but a non-proportional constant, so they are parallel and distinct — no solution.
